description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7.5 agentic cluster: agent identity architecture (ASI03) vs credential custody, per-tool enforcement, SDLC standing authority, and end-user RBAC.

cases.prompt: Design what identities our agents and sub-agents should run as, how a user's authority is delegated through them, and where privilege could escalate.
cases.reason: Agent identity models, delegation attenuation, and escalation hunting are ASI03; custody of the resulting credentials is the hardener, and end-user roles×permissions is the RBAC designer.

cases.prompt: Our agent's API keys are in a client-visible env var and haven't been rotated in a year — fix the secret handling.
cases.reason: Exposure, storage, and rotation of secret material is credential custody (the manual-only fixer); the identity reviewer decides what identities/scopes should exist.

cases.prompt: Make sure each tool the agent calls executes with the calling user's permissions and validates its arguments first.
cases.reason: Binding individual tool calls to the calling user's authority with argument validation is the LLM06/ASI02 tool boundary; the identity reviewer covers the fleet-level identity and delegation model those checks enforce against.

cases.prompt: Define what our coding agent may commit, merge, or deploy on its own versus what always needs a named human.
cases.reason: Standing agent-vs-human authority over SDLC actions (merge/deploy/auto-merge) is the Phase 1.5 governance matrix; ASI03 reviews runtime agent identities and privilege paths in the product system.

cases.prompt: Design the roles and permissions matrix for our SaaS users — admins, managers, members — across our resources.
cases.reason: Human end-user RBAC is the Phase 3 matrix designer; the agent identity reviewer consumes it as the ceiling that delegated agent authority must respect.

cases.prompt: Verify that our agents actually authenticate each other's messages and that a rogue process can't spoof agent-to-agent traffic.
cases.reason: Message-layer authentication, integrity, and replay are ASI07 comms review; the identity reviewer defines the identities that authentication uses, not the transport security itself.
